critical.mandate: Use structured questioning techniques from methods.csv
Document all requirements discovered with clear traceability
Distinguish between stated needs, implied needs, and assumed needs

step.title: Context Gathering
step.ask: What product or feature do you need to elicit requirements for?
What existing documentation is available (PRD, briefs, notes)?

step.title: Method Selection
step.action: Load methods.csv to show available elicitation techniques
Present available methods with their descriptions and best-fit scenarios
step.ask: Which elicitation method(s) would you like to use? (or let me recommend based on your context)
step.action: If user defers: recommend 2-3 methods based on the project context

step.title: Elicitation Execution
step.action: For each selected method: execute the structured questioning flow
5 Whys: ask "why" iteratively to uncover root motivations
Socratic Method: challenge assumptions through guided questions
User Story Mapping: walk through the user journey end-to-end
MoSCoW: classify each requirement as Must/Should/Could/Won't
Kano Model: categorize features by satisfaction impact
Jobs-to-be-Done: identify the core job the user is hiring the product for
Assumption Mapping: list and validate all project assumptions
Stakeholder Mapping: identify all stakeholders and their needs
Document all requirements discovered during each method

step.title: Requirements Synthesis
step.action: Consolidate all discovered requirements across methods
Categorize as: functional, non-functional, constraint, assumption
Tag each requirement with source method and confidence level
Identify gaps where further elicitation is needed

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate elicitation report with: context summary, methods used, all discovered requirements (categorized), assumptions log, gaps identified, and recommended next steps.
